product_overview: SonarDiff is a website change-monitoring backend that lets users register accounts, create monitors for target URLs, and receive accurate diff-based email alerts when monitored content changes. It supports tiered scraping, selector-based extraction, exclusion rules, quota enforcement, and dashboard visibility for recent changes.
core_goals: Provide reliable authentication and session management for users
Allow users to create and manage monitors for specific URLs
Detect meaningful page changes using tiered scraping and diff thresholds
Support CSS selector targeting and content exclusion to reduce noise
Deliver email alerts when changes exceed the configured threshold
Track usage quotas and enforce plan limits transparently
Expose dashboard and diff history endpoints for monitoring insight

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Users can register, log in, manage their profile, generate API tokens, and delete their account using session-based authentication.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/register with valid email, password, name, and optional plan -> Receive 201 with token and user object -> GET /api/auth/me with session token -> Receive 200 with current user profile and preferences
POST /api/auth/login with valid email and password -> Receive 200 with token and user object -> POST /api/auth/logout with session token -> Receive 200 with { success: true }
POST /api/auth/register with an email that is already in use -> Receive 409 with { error: 'Email already registered' }
POST /api/auth/login with invalid credentials -> Receive 401 with { error: 'Invalid email or password' }
GET /api/auth/me without a valid session cookie -> Receive 401 with { error: 'Authentication required' }
PATCH /api/auth/me with updated name, email, password, or preferences -> Receive 200 with updated user object
POST /api/auth/api-token with a valid session cookie -> Receive 200 with a newly generated apiToken
DELETE /api/auth/me with a valid session cookie -> Receive 200 with { success: true } and account data removed

features.name: Monitor Management
features.description: Users can create, update, view, delete, and manually check website monitors, as well as inspect change diffs and diff details.
user_flows: POST /api/monitors with a valid session cookie and monitor data -> Receive 201 with created monitor -> GET /api/monitors -> Receive 200 with the new monitor in the user's list -> GET /api/monitors/:id -> Receive 200 with the monitor details
POST /api/monitors with a plan-compliant URL, interval, threshold, and optional Pro-only selector/exclusion rules -> Receive 201 with monitor object
POST /api/monitors with invalid data such as malformed URL or out-of-range threshold -> Receive 400 with { error: string }
POST /api/monitors with Pro-only fields on a Free plan -> Receive 403 with { error: string }
PATCH /api/monitors/:id with updated name, URL, interval, active state, threshold, or Pro-only fields -> Receive 200 with updated monitor
PATCH /api/monitors/:id for a monitor the user does not own or that does not exist -> Receive 404 with { error: 'Monitor not found' }
DELETE /api/monitors/:id for an existing owned monitor -> Receive 200 with { success: true } and cascading removal of related snapshots, diffs, and alerts
DELETE /api/monitors/:id for a missing monitor -> Receive 404 with { error: 'Monitor not found' }
POST /api/monitors/:id/check on a Pro plan monitor -> Receive 200 with { success: true, message: string, manualChecksUsedThisPeriod: number }
POST /api/monitors/:id/check on a Free plan monitor -> Receive 403 with { error: string }
GET /api/monitors/:id/diffs -> Receive 200 with diff history for that monitor
GET /api/monitors/:id/diffs/:diffId -> Receive 200 with full diff and monitor metadata
GET /api/monitors/:id/diffs for a nonexistent monitor -> Receive 404 with { error: 'Monitor not found' }

features.name: Dashboard Stats
features.description: Users can view aggregated dashboard data including active monitor count and recent diffs.
user_flows: GET /api/dashboard/stats with a valid session cookie -> Receive 200 with activeMonitors and recentDiffs
GET /api/dashboard/stats without authentication -> Receive 401 from the protected route guard

features.name: DOM Picker
features.description: Users can preview how a CSS selector behaves on a target URL before creating a monitor.
user_flows: POST /api/dom-picker/preview with a valid session cookie, URL, and CSS selector -> Receive 200 with extractedText and httpStatus

POST /api/dom-picker/preview with a valid URL but missing or invalid selector -> Receive 200 with empty or partial extractedText when the selector resolves, or 400 with { error: string } if the request cannot be processed
POST /api/dom-picker/preview with malformed URL or scrape failure -> Receive 400 with { error: string }

features.name: Tiered Scraper Worker
features.description: Background jobs scrape monitored pages using tiered strategies, store snapshots, update remembered fetcher tiers, compute diffs, and queue alerts.
user_flows: Create or update a monitor through /api/monitors so a scrape job is enqueued -> Worker fetches the URL using the stored or auto-detected tier -> Worker stores snapshot content and diff HTML in the database -> Worker updates monitor.fetcher_tier and last_checked_at -> If change threshold is exceeded, Worker queues an alert job

Worker attempts Tier 1 HTTP scraping and receives placeholder or low-text content -> Worker escalates to Tier 2 headless scraping -> If Tier 2 fails due to bot protection, Worker escalates to Tier 3 stealth scraping -> Worker stores the successful tier and snapshot

Worker cannot find the CSS selector on a monitored page -> Worker attempts adaptive fingerprint relocation -> If relocation succeeds, Worker updates element_fingerprint and continues -> If relocation fails, Worker marks the monitor unreachable and stops the scrape path

features.name: Alert System
features.description: When a monitored page changes beyond threshold, email alerts are generated from pre-rendered diff HTML and sent through Resend.
user_flows: Worker inserts an alert job after a snapshot change exceeds the configured threshold -> Alert worker loads snapshot.diff_html and monitor details -> Alert worker sends email through Resend -> Alert status is updated to sent
Alert worker processes a queued alert but snapshot or monitor data cannot be found -> Alert worker returns an error path and the alert can be marked failed by downstream handling
Worker detects a distinct unreachable condition for a monitor -> Alert system sends a specific email indicating the element could not be recovered

features.name: Quota Management
features.description: The system tracks monthly checks per user, enforces plan quotas, and updates usage atomically.
user_flows: POST /api/monitors/:id/check with a valid session cookie and available quota -> Receive 200 from the manual check endpoint -> quota_usage is incremented for the current month
POST /api/monitors with a new monitor that would exceed projected quota limits -> Receive 403 with { error: string } and monitor creation is blocked
POST /api/monitors/:id/check when monthly limits are exhausted -> Receive 403 with { error: string }
Monthly reset cron runs on the first day of the month -> A new quota_usage row is created or carried forward for active users -> Subsequent checks use the new month bucket

tech_stack.auth: session cookie (crypto.randomBytes token stored in sessions table)

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Register, login, logout, session management, profile update, account deletion, API token generation

endpoints.description: Create a new user account and return session token

response_schema.201: { token: string, user: { id, email, name, plan, planLimit, checksUsedThisPeriod } }

endpoints.description: Authenticate with email and password, return session token

response_schema.200: { token: string, user: { id, email, name, plan, planLimit, checksUsedThisPeriod } }
{ user: { id, email, name, plan, planLimit, checksUsedThisPeriod, periodResetAt, preferences, apiToken, createdAt } }

endpoints.description: Update profile fields (name, email, password, preferences)
Delete account and cascade all user data
Generate a new API token for the current user

features.name: Monitor Management
features.description: CRUD for website monitors, manual check triggering, diff and snapshot history

endpoints.description: List all monitors for the authenticated user
Create a new monitor. Validates plan limits and quota projections.
Get a single monitor by ID (ownership enforced)
Update monitor fields. Validates plan, quota, and threshold range.
Delete a monitor (cascades to snapshots, diffs, alerts)
Trigger an immediate manual check (Pro plan only, 50/month limit)

response_schema.200: { success: true, message: string, manualChecksUsedThisPeriod: number }

endpoints.description: List all diffs detected for a monitor
Get full diff detail including snapshot metadata and diff data

features.name: Dashboard Stats
features.description: Aggregated statistics for the user dashboard

endpoints.description: Returns active monitor count and 10 most recent diffs

features.name: DOM Picker
features.description: Preview endpoint for testing CSS selectors on a URL before creating a monitor

endpoints.description: Scrape a URL and return extracted text for a given CSS selector

features.name: Tiered Scraper Worker
features.description: BullMQ worker that scrapes monitors using a 3-tier strategy (HTTP, headless, stealth). Saves snapshots with content to DB, updates fetcher_tier, runs diff engine, queues alerts.

features.name: Alert System
features.description: Email alerts via Resend when monitored content changes beyond threshold. Pre-rendered diff HTML stored in snapshots.diff_html.

features.name: Quota Management
features.description: Atomic quota gate per user using checksUsedThisPeriod. Monthly reset cron on 1st of month.

known_limitations.issue: threshold field in POST /api/monitors has no range validation prior to this upgrade (fixed in current codebase)
known_limitations.impact: Values outside 0–1 could previously be stored

known_limitations.issue: Manual check endpoint is Pro-only; no fallback for Free users
known_limitations.impact: Free users receive 403 when triggering manual checks

known_limitations.issue: Diff file still written to disk (dual-write phase); full removal deferred to Phase 9
known_limitations.impact: Disk usage grows until Phase 9 cleanup

known_limitations.issue: Auth uses bcrypt sessions (not Supabase magic link); Supabase migration is Phase 7
